title: After-Action Report — native-stat audit + the runner-advancement correction
scope: audit of the analytics/stats layer for imported MLB constants, two doc/comment corrections, and — on the owner's call — a coordinated wOBAScale recalibration (MLB 1.20 → O27-native 0.86) in wRC+/VORP, with runs-per-win empirically validated and replacement level kept. Spans two commits (audit + corrections first, then the wOBAScale switch).

rationale.item: Follow-up to the BSR native-ification. The brief was "do the native stat audit and fix speed-based runner advancement."

app.py hardcoded wOBAScale = 1.20 (FanGraphs convention) for wRC+ and VORP. The O27-native value is the OBP-scale factor that derive_linear_weights already fits; on the recon DB it's 0.8589, not 1.20 (O27's league wOBA is 0.489 vs MLB's ~0.32 — a different run environment, where dividing by 1.20 mis-converts wOBA points to runs).

delegation_events.decision: Switch wOBAScale to O27-native 0.86 with coordinated re-checks of runs-per-win and replacement level
delegation_events.quote: The owner chose to go native **with coordinated re-checks of runs-per-win and replacement level** (the worry being that 1.20→0.86 multiplies VORP ~1.40× and so inflates WAR).

delegation_events.decision: Optional speed-advancement flavour tuning left undecided

actions.description: Corrected o27/engine/baserunning.py module docstring: the stale "Phase 2: will add probabilistic runner advancement" note now points to the real speed-aware producer in prob.py.
Added a correction box to the BSR AAR and fixed the follow-up that called speed-based advancement an unbuilt gap.
derive_linear_weights() now returns woba_scale (the native OBP-scale factor, ≈0.86).
wRC+ and bVORP now use the native woba_scale instead of the hardcoded MLB 1.20. This is the one metric-output change: WAR widens toward better reconciliation (top 6.2→8.7), wRC+ centering unchanged.

Audited the stat suite for remaining MLB constants and confirmed what is already O27-native (wOBA linear weights, FIP/DIPS constant, runs-per-win, RE24, BSR, Pythag exponent, BaseRuns coefficients, Game Score coeffs, fielding run value, WPA/Leverage).

risk_posture: The rest are documented with measured tradeoffs, not blind-fixed.
Replacement stays at 0.85×league. Net (72) lands slightly under the fuzzy ~94 target; lowering replacement to close the gap would only push tops higher, so 0.85 is the conservative keep.
**Recommendation:** no fix needed. *Optional* flavour tuning ... is available, but it has ~0 WAR/BSR impact and risks disturbing the existing calibration/tests.

validation_claims.claim: Re-verified with correct column aliases: PA-weighted player wOBA 0.5054 ≈ league OBP 0.4882; wRC+ centers at 98; WAR top-5 [6.2, 4.7, 4.5, 4.4, 4.3].
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run the aggregation against the recon DB with correct column names

validation_claims.claim: runner_advances_for_hit confirmed as the live producer (call sites prob.py:2103, :2873); _resolve_table receives a real per-runner speed_dev at every single/double/triple call site.
validation_claims.check_method: Inspect o27/engine/prob.py call sites and _resolve_table arguments

validation_claims.claim: Fitting RPW empirically from actual team W–L vs run differential gives 19.89, essentially identical to the derived 20.05.
validation_claims.check_method: Refit run_diff / wins-above-.500 through origin on the same DB

validation_claims.claim: By the proper WAR reconciliation (Σ net position WAR ≈ league wins-above-replacement ≈ 94), the old 1.20 netted only 49; native nets 72, closer to target.
validation_claims.check_method: Recompute Σ net position WAR under both scales on the recon DB

non_actions.reason: XO's entire purpose is a reading layer that maps an O27 z-score onto an MLB-readable mean±sd; the MLB anchors are the intended target scale (there's even a "LOCKED DECISION" comment). Replacing them would defeat the metric.

non_actions.item: League-baseline wOBA weights (app.py:2121, MLB) re-centering of wRC+
non_actions.reason: it also lowers replacement_woba = 0.85×league_woba, which further raises WAR (compounding with 3b). Left as a separate, measured decision rather than bundled into this recalibration. Low urgency: regulars already read a sane ~97.

Because the FIP constant is refit so league FIP = league ERA, coefficient miscalibration is largely absorbed into the constant; only the spread between pitchers could shift. A proper refit (à la BaseRuns) is a contained future project, not demonstrably broken today. Documented, not fixed.

non_actions.item: Positional DRS ranges, wERA arc weights, replacement levels (0.85/1.20)
non_actions.reason: Each could be empirically fit from O27 distributions; none is demonstrably wrong. Lower priority; listed for completeness.
it has ~0 WAR/BSR impact and risks disturbing the existing calibration/tests. Left to the owner's call.
Runs-per-win is already correct (empirical 19.89 vs derived 20.05). No change.

residual_risks: wRC+ now centers at ~91 full-pop / 97 for regulars instead of exactly 100 because the league-baseline wOBA still uses MLB weights (~5% gap).
Net position WAR (72) lands slightly under the fuzzy ~94 reconciliation target.
open_hooks: League-baseline wOBA via native weights (§3c follow-up)
FIP/DIPS coefficient refit à la BaseRuns
Empirical fits for positional DRS ranges, wERA arc weights, replacement levels
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — native-stat audit + the runner-advancement correction
Finding 2 — what's already O27-native (do not touch)
Finding 3 — remaining MLB constants, each a judgement call (NOT blind-fixed)
3e. Positional DRS ranges, wERA arc weights, replacement levels (0.85/1.20)
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/war-oaa-reconciliation-koalas-7t0pG. | Process note: during the audit I briefly mis-concluded that wRC+/WAR were badly broken (wRC+ centering at −10). That was a bug in my throwaway test harness (it aliased SQL columns as hits/doubles where the aggregator reads h/d2). Re-run with the real column names, wRC+ centers at 98 and WAR tops out at a reasonable 6.2 — production is fine. | Finding 1 detail: producer is o27/engine/prob.py:runner_advances_for_hit() (lines ~1507-1691); _runner_advance() (lines ~1022-1067) adds a speed/baserunning-driven extra-base attempt with a TOOTBLAN check; config knobs cfg.SPEED_ADVANCE_MOD (0.12), ARM_ADVANCE_MOD (0.11), RUNNER_EXTRA_SPEED_SCALE (0.35), TOOTBLAN_*; o27v2/sim.py:432 normalises 20-80 ratings via _scout.to_unit() so no scale-mismatch bug; apply_park_effects not called in prob.py/pa.py so the 14 fixed runner_advances tuples in park_effects.py don't shadow the speed-aware result. Bases-gained-per-opportunity correlates only ~+0.05 with speed — dilution, not absence (most opportunities resolve by force/context; an extra base is worth ~0 runs in O27). | 3b supporting points: high top-end structurally plausible — team wins span 17–52 of 66 games, so a stacked team carries ~32 WAR and an ~8.7-WAR star is believable; net effect — wRC+ centering unchanged (regulars 98→97), wRC+/VORP now share one O27-native scale. | Honest notes: I was wrong earlier that runner advancement ignored speed — root cause: trusting a consumer-side docstring; lesson: trace the producer. I nearly shipped a false "wRC+ is broken" alarm off a harness-only bug; absolute-value surprises get re-derived through the real code path before they become claims. The suite needed far less fixing than the brief assumed.
parser_notes: Audit-style AAR with explicit self-corrections of earlier claims; scope statement given verbatim in the header block.
